The project is divided into 5 phases with man-hours in geometric progression and a common ratio of 2. The total man-hours required for the project is 310. Let the first phase require x man-hours. Then the sequence is x, 2x, 4x, 8x, 16x. Summing these values gives x + 2x + 4x + 8x + 16x = 31x. Since 31x equals 310, x is 10. Therefore the man-hours for each phase are 10, 20, 40, 80, and 160.

The initial timeline for the project is 20 weeks. The project manager realizes that each phase will take 1.5 times longer than planned. The original duration for each phase is calculated by dividing the man-hours by the number of workers. However, since the number of workers is not provided, we assume the original duration is distributed evenly across the phases. The total duration is 20 weeks, so each phase originally took 4 weeks. With the increase in time, each phase will now take 6 weeks. The new total project duration is 5 phases multiplied by 6 weeks per phase. The new total project duration is 30 weeks.

The project manager must adjust the schedule to accommodate the increased duration. This adjustment requires re-planning and possibly reallocating resources. The project manager will communicate the changes to the teams to ensure everyone is aware of the new timeline. The project manager will also monitor progress closely to ensure that the project stays on track. The project manager will review the updated schedule to confirm that all tasks are accounted for and that the new timeline is feasible. The project manager will also consider any potential risks that may arise due to the extended timeline. The project manager will ensure that the teams have the necessary support and resources to complete their tasks within the new timeframe. The project manager will maintain open lines of communication with all stakeholders to keep them informed of the project's progress. The project manager will also provide regular updates to the project sponsor to ensure transparency. The project manager will continue to track the project's performance against the updated schedule. The project manager will ensure that all deliverables are met within the new timeline. The project manager will also be prepared to make further adjustments if needed.
